channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump sets deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has given Iran until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or the U.S. will hit Iran's power plants. Iran has said it won't back down.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has given Iran until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or the U.S. will hit Iran's power plants. Iran has said it won't back down.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Former Navy fighter pilot talks about US mission to rescue pilot shot down over Iran
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Navy fighter pilot Matthew "Whiz" Buckley about the U.S. rescue mission for the crew members of an F-15 fighter jet shot down over Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Navy fighter pilot Matthew "Whiz" Buckley about the U.S. rescue mission for the crew members of an F-15 fighter jet shot down over Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Shingles can hit younger than you think. The vaccine can prevent the painful illness
item.description: A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.

(Image credit: triocean/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Milne-Tyte

item.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to the 'Today' show months after her mother's disappearance
item.description: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.
item.content:encoded: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump says Iran has until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump is in a tight corner politically as he ramps up Iran war messaging, Artemis II crew readies for lunar flyby.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran has until Tuesday night to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump is in a tight corner politically as he ramps up Iran war messaging, Artemis II crew readies for lunar flyby.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Raye talks about her artistic journey and new album, 'This Music May Contain Hope'
item.description: Following in the footsteps of Amy Winehouse and Adele, Raye is among a group of British women who've invaded the U.S. pop charts. Her latest album is called "This Music May Contain Hope."
item.content:encoded: Following in the footsteps of Amy Winehouse and Adele, Raye is among a group of British women who've invaded the U.S. pop charts. Her latest album is called "This Music May Contain Hope."
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Environmentalists want 'Alligator Alcatraz' to close. They'll get a hearing this week
item.description: Environmental groups in Florida will get a hearing in federal court this week over whether the ICE facility dubbed Alligator Alcatraz needs to close because of environmental damage to the Everglades.
item.content:encoded: Environmental groups in Florida will get a hearing in federal court this week over whether the ICE facility dubbed Alligator Alcatraz needs to close because of environmental damage to the Everglades.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bowman

item.title: Pope Leo calls for peace and an end to world conflicts in first Easter Mass
item.description: Pope Leo delivered his first Easter Mass as pope with a call for world leaders to choose peace over war.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo delivered his first Easter Mass as pope with a call for world leaders to choose peace over war.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Former Vatican correspondent discusses Pope Leo's Easter message of peace
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Vatican correspondent and Georgetown scholar Christopher White about Pope Leo's Easter peace message.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former Vatican correspondent and Georgetown scholar Christopher White about Pope Leo's Easter peace message.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: As Trump ramps up his Iran war messaging, he remains in a tight spot politically
item.description: President Trump is praising the military for a risky rescue mission in Iran. But even with military successes, Trump remains in a political bind.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is praising the military for a risky rescue mission in Iran. But even with military successes, Trump remains in a political bind.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson
